07_configuration_geometry_and_sampling.title: Configuration Geometry And Sampling
07_configuration_geometry_and_sampling.subtitle: The configuration knobs are physical sampling choices on the aperture: how many spider-web lines are kept, and therefore how many bead-pull points survive per gap.

08_gap_and_z_coverage.title: Gap And z Coverage
08_gap_and_z_coverage.subtitle: The four discovered gaps occupy distinct z regions. `gap_count` chooses how many of those regions are included, while `z_slice_count` chooses how many sampled z positions are retained inside each included gap.

09_bead_pull_physics_chain.title: Bead-Pull Physics Chain
09_bead_pull_physics_chain.subtitle: The reduced reconstruction starts from the measured complex reflection map, isolates the bead response in time, removes the slowly varying baseline, and converts the residual into a field-sensitive quantity.

top_chain.title: Measured Response
lines: $S_{11}(x,y,z,\nu)$ is measured on the full aperture grid.
Each map combines cavity response, background, and the localized bead perturbation.

formula_block: $G(t)$ is a cosine-edged window of width $t_{\mathrm{width}}$ centered on the predicted bead return.
The reduced line products are phase/sign matched against the full-grid $\sqrt{\Delta\gamma}$ before the field conversion is applied.

10_mode_model_and_fit_parameters.title: Mode Model And Fit Parameters
10_mode_model_and_fit_parameters.subtitle: The reconstruction uses a Hermite-Gaussian-inspired beam prior. The diagnostics visualize low-order HG building blocks, while the current fit uses a fundamental HG00-like envelope with curvature and global phase.
model_formula: In the present fit, the modal weights are fixed to HG00, so $\mathrm{HG}(x,y)=1$ in the fitted model.
parameter_meaning: $x_0, y_0$: beam center on the aperture
$A$: complex field scale after absorbing the best-fit global factor
The parameter vector is fitted independently for every retained $(z,\nu)$ slice.

11_complex_ET_reconstruction.title: Complex ET Reconstruction
11_complex_ET_reconstruction.subtitle: The physics target is the complex transverse field integral. Strong and stressed configurations can be compared directly against the full-grid reference in both the complex plane and along z.

12_configuration_tradeoffs_for_physics.title: Configuration Tradeoffs For Physics
12_configuration_tradeoffs_for_physics.subtitle: The dominant tradeoff is physical coverage versus fidelity: more spider lines help, while extending the reconstruction across more gap regions stresses the reduced model strongly.
12_configuration_tradeoffs_for_physics.takeaway: For this dataset, the best overall reconstruction comes from the densest in-plane sampling but only the highest-z gap. Multi-gap reconstructions are much harder: even the best multi-gap option is far worse than the single-gap optimum, and the fully covered 4-gap / 3-slice configuration remains a clear stress test rather than a precision configuration.
